title: shonia tristigma subsp. parvifolia
intro: Shonia tristigma subsp. parvifolia, also known as the African stinkwood or stink wood, is a plant species belonging to the family Sapindaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory problems, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional African medicine. Shonia tristigma subsp. parvifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
